value.found_quote: In Antigravity 2.0, agents work in **Projects** (previously in Agent Manager, agents were strictly mapped to a single workspace folder). * **Worktree Support**: Projects natively support Git worktrees, allowing agents to operate in isolated background folders. * **Scoped Settings**: Settings are scoped... * **Scoped Permissions**: Attach permission grants to projects... Start quick, one-off conversations outside of any Project. These sessions run in an isolated local scratch folder.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes Projects in Antigravity 2.0, including agents working in Projects, folder/repository boundaries via worktrees and multi-folder access, isolated background folders, scoped settings, scoped permissions, and one-off conversations running in an isolated local scratch folder. All claim elements are substantiated as current features.
